name: Agent Evaluation Platform
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Evaluation inference — LLM-as-judge scoring for groundedness, relevance, coherence, fluency, and safety; baseline agent execution for benchmark comparisons; prompt variation generation for A/B testing; automated test case synthesis from evaluation criteria

prod.note: ~3M tokens/day, gpt-4o for judge + gpt-4o-mini for synthesis

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for high-volume batch evaluations

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Evaluation runtime — benchmark execution engine, agent sandboxing for isolated test runs, A/B test traffic splitting, leaderboard API, human scoring UI backend, evaluation pipeline orchestration, result aggregation workers

prod.note: 4 vCPU, 8GB, min 3 replicas for concurrent evals

enterprise.note: 8 vCPU, 16GB, multi-region, 10+ replicas, GPU for embedding evals

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Evaluation store — benchmark definitions and test suites, evaluation run results with per-metric scores, agent configurations and versions, leaderboard rankings with historical tracking, human scoring annotations, A/B test configurations and outcomes, evaluation metadata and lineage

services.name: Azure Machine Learning
services.purpose: Evaluation pipelines — batch evaluation orchestration, dataset management for test suites, statistical analysis of evaluation results, metric computation (BLEU, ROUGE, BERTScore, custom metrics), drift detection across evaluation runs

dev.note: Free tier, shared compute for pipeline development

prod.note: Dedicated compute clusters for batch evaluations

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Evaluation triggers — scheduled benchmark runs, webhook integrations for CI/CD pipeline evaluation gates, human scoring task distribution, leaderboard recalculation, notification delivery (Slack, Teams, email), evaluation report generation

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: OpenAI API keys, agent endpoint credentials, CI/CD webhook secrets, notification service tokens, evaluation dataset encryption keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Platform KPIs — evaluation pipeline latency, agent response times under test, scoring consistency metrics, human annotator inter-rater reliability, leaderboard query performance, A/B test statistical significance tracking

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini as LLM-judge for initial screening passes (coherence, fluency, format compliance) and reserve GPT-4o for nuanced evaluations (groundedness, safety, reasoning quality) — saving 70% on evaluation inference costs for routine benchmarks

Batch evaluation runs during off-peak hours — schedule nightly evaluation pipelines when Azure OpenAI quota utilization is lowest; most evaluation results don't need real-time processing, and batch mode reduces per-token costs

Cache evaluation results in Cosmos DB with composite keys (agent_version + benchmark_id + test_case_hash) — identical agent versions evaluated against the same benchmark don't need re-evaluation; invalidate cache only when agent or benchmark changes

Implement progressive evaluation — run cheap metrics first (format compliance, response length, keyword presence) and only advance passing candidates to expensive LLM-judge scoring; eliminates 40-60% of costly evaluations for clearly failing agents
Use Container Apps scale-to-zero for evaluation workers — evaluation workloads are bursty (nightly runs, CI/CD triggers); consumption plan eliminates idle compute costs between evaluation windows

Downsample test suites for development iterations — run full benchmark suites only for release candidates; use statistically representative 10% samples during development for fast feedback at 90% cost reduction
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
